scope: Offline structural fixture only. This deliberately did not flash COM36 because the prior minimal P1.6 visual proof is already live-proven and this slice only broadens text/button/number object ordering.

structural_evidence.reorder_shape: The button is object id 2, while its referenced number target is the later object id 5. Additional text objects occupy ids 1, 3, and 4.
structural_evidence.compiled_event_target: The pytest fixture recompiles incbtn's event table against the rebuilt object list and asserts the numeric ++ bytecode points at the rebuilt numval local user slot, not a stale seed object.

conclusion.offline_reorder_broadening: Closed for the smallest low-risk slice: a full-page rebuild can reorder supported text/button/number objects, preserve the known-good forward button event, delete seed objects, patch the verified GB2312 font, and emit a valid checksum without flashing.
conclusion.live_boundary: No new hardware claim is made here. The latest live panel proof remains examples/number_demo/full_page_rebuild_hardware_verified_2026-05-16.json.
